summary: Connect a ButtonUnfilled trigger to an anchored Menu for a compact command or view-selection popup without application-authored positioning.
useWhen: A button should reveal a short contextual collection of commands.
A compact button should show the current view or mode and let the user choose another one from a menu.
avoidWhen: The choice is a submitted form value with a field label or validation; use Select.
The available local views can remain visible; use TabGroup.
The choices are primary or secondary application destinations; use the matching navigation component.
The popup contains substantial content, confirmation, or a form; use Modal.

components.role: Provides the visible trigger, popup relationship attributes, and focus target.
Owns anchored placement, menu semantics, keyboard navigation, viewport clamping, selection, and close behavior.

stateOwnership: The application owns the selected command or view value and derives the trigger label and each item's isSelected state from it.
The application synchronizes one open boolean to ButtonUnfilled.expanded and Menu.open.
Menu owns popup placement and keyboard navigation; the application never calculates popup coordinates or applies positional CSS.
On dsSelect, update the selected value, close the Menu, and restore focus to the trigger.
On dsClose, synchronize the application open state to false.
accessibility: Set ButtonUnfilled.haspopup to menu, controls to the Menu id, and expanded from the same open state passed to Menu.
Give Menu a concise menuLabel describing the available commands or views.
Set Menu.initialFocusVisible only when the trigger MouseEvent detail is zero, indicating keyboard activation.
Keep visible selected styling synchronized with the application's selected value; Menu exposes the selected command through aria-current.
Return focus to the trigger after selection; Menu already restores it for Escape and outside dismissal.
responsiveBehavior: Keep Menu's default choice-cell anchor alignment unless deliberate custom frame geometry is required.
Let Menu clamp to the viewport and avoid application-authored fixed or absolute positioning.
Use a concise trigger label that can fit the owning layout without hiding the selected meaning.

recipes.title: Selected view menu
recipes.summary: A ButtonUnfilled displays the active view and opens a Menu whose selected row updates that view.

files.content:
<ds-button-unfilled
  id="view-menu-trigger"
  label="View · Overview"
  haspopup="menu"
  controls="view-menu"
></ds-button-unfilled>
<ds-menu
  id="view-menu"
  anchor-id="view-menu-trigger"
  menu-label="Choose view"
></ds-menu>

import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-button-unfilled.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-menu.js'
import type { MenuItemData } from '@ds-mo/ui'

const choices: MenuItemData[] = [
  { label: 'Overview', value: 'overview' },
  { label: 'Map', value: 'map' },
  { label: 'Timeline', value: 'timeline' },
]

const trigger = document.querySelector<HTMLDsButtonUnfilledElement>('#view-menu-trigger')!
const menu = document.querySelector<HTMLDsMenuElement>('#view-menu')!
let selectedValue = 'overview'

function render() {
  const selected = choices.find(item => item.value === selectedValue) ?? choices[0]
  trigger.label = `View · ${selected.label}`
  menu.items = choices.map(item => ({ ...item, isSelected: item.value === selectedValue }))
}

function setOpen(open: boolean) {
  trigger.expanded = open
  menu.open = open
}

trigger.addEventListener('dsClick', event => {
  const mouseEvent = (event as CustomEvent<MouseEvent>).detail
  menu.initialFocusVisible = mouseEvent.detail === 0
  setOpen(!menu.open)
})
menu.addEventListener('dsClose', () => setOpen(false))
menu.addEventListener('dsSelect', event => {
  selectedValue = (event as CustomEvent<MenuItemData>).detail.value ?? selectedValue
  render()
  setOpen(false)
  requestAnimationFrame(() => trigger.setFocus())
})

render()

notes: Assign Menu.items as a JavaScript property; complex arrays cannot be expressed as an HTML attribute.
Do not add positioning styles to either element.

recipes.title: Selected view menu
recipes.summary: A controlled React composition using generated ButtonUnfilled and Menu wrappers.
notes: Use one React state value for both the trigger expanded prop and Menu open prop.
Keep the stable trigger and menu ids unique when rendering more than one composition.

recipes.title: Selected view menu
recipes.summary: A controlled Angular composition using generated standalone ButtonUnfilled and Menu adapters.

files.content:
<ds-button-unfilled
  id="view-menu-trigger"
  [label]="'View · ' + selectedLabel"
  haspopup="menu"
  controls="view-menu"
  [expanded]="open"
  (dsClick)="toggleMenu($event)"
></ds-button-unfilled>
<ds-menu
  id="view-menu"
  anchorId="view-menu-trigger"
  menuLabel="Choose view"
  [items]="menuItems"
  [open]="open"
  [initialFocusVisible]="initialFocusVisible"
  (dsClose)="open = false"
  (dsSelect)="selectView($event)"
></ds-menu>

notes: Bind items as a property and import both generated standalone adapters.
Keep the stable trigger and menu ids unique when rendering more than one composition.
